description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a raised stone wall forming the well shaft. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof covered in reddish-brown, fish-scale patterned shingles, supported by two sturdy wooden posts. A horizontal wooden crank handle is mounted across the front, connected to a spindle mechanism for drawing water, with a visible rope extending down into the well. The stonework is rough and irregular, giving the structure a rustic, hand-built appearance, and the base is surrounded by a circular stone-paved platform. The wood appears weathered, and the overall construction suggests durability and functionality, suitable for outdoor urban or park settings. No decorative elements are present, emphasizing a utilitarian design.
description: A rustic stone water well with a pitched wooden shingle roof, crank handle, and stone-paved base. The design is functional and sturdy, with visible rope and spindle for drawing water.
description_view_0: Front view showing the shingled roof, wooden crank handle, and open well shaft with stone base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular wooden roof profile and the thickness of the stone wall.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the roof, stone well, and crank handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the spindle mechanism and rope, as well as the stonework details.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A horizontal wooden crank handle and spindle mechanism with a rope are present for drawing water.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high surrounds the well opening, with a pitched wooden roof supported by two posts.
